title: House on Haunted Hill

evidence.note: William Castle's 1959 Vincent Price vehicle was not renewed and has circulated as US public domain for decades across archives and budget labels. Primary renewal-search scan not yet attached — held at 'likely'.
Von Dexter score; separate registration status not verified here.
Original screenplay by Robb White; no underlying novel or play.
No known active marks; the 1999 remake is separately protected.
Modern HD restorations and colorized versions carry new protectable elements.

editorial:
William Castle's gimmick masterpiece — the one he screened with a plastic skeleton flying over the audience — lapsed into the public domain and became one of the most re-released horror films of all time. Vincent Price hosting a haunted-house party with a murder mystery inside: the formula still works.

Original screenplay, no franchise marks, and the usual two cautions of its era: an unverified score chain, and modern restorations that are separately protected.
faq: Can I upload or reuse House on Haunted Hill?
Archival prints carry low risk; the 1999 remake and modern restorations are protected and must be avoided as sources.
Why are so many Vincent Price films public domain?
Independent producers of the era frequently missed renewal deadlines — 28 years after release, someone had to remember to file. Many didn't.
